stop_condition: All 3 trials in ~/Documents/aic/aic_engine/config/sample_config.yaml pass under unmodified CheatCode.py against Isaac Sim with the same per-trial outcome (insertion_event fires, no off_limit contacts) that Gazebo produces. parity_report.py regenerates a deterministic side-by-side table proving it. .planning/.m1_shipped touched after the report is green.
scope_note: Hardware caveat acknowledged — running Isaac Sim + Gazebo concurrently may stress GPU/CPU and degrade physics realtime factor. M1 stop condition is correctness of trial outcomes (same insertion vs off_limit verdicts), not realtime parity. Spec-machine upgrade is the operator's path for any realtime delta.
out_of_scope_for_m1: Cable physics decorative-only (audit finding #5) — defer to M2; cable is rigidly attached to gripper (attach_cable_to_gripper=True) so cable physics doesn't dynamically affect trials. Scoring is contact-based on plug-end geometry. Density is 7 orders of magnitude off realistic; revisit only if a future trial type requires cable to drag on environment.

Phase 1 gap-closure plans 01-G01 (mount-rail visual scale diagnosis) and 01-G02 (apply verdict-specific fix) — latent visual polish, not ship-blockers. Plan 01-G05's setup_wrist_cameras prim path correction is absorbed into wrist-cameras-restore below.

feedback_loops.audit_dx02: python3 exts/aic-dt/scripts/audit_dx02.py — checks 4-surface contract on every MCP atom; must exit 0
feedback_loops.verify_phase_1: bash exts/aic-dt/scripts/verify_phase_1.sh — Phase 1 runtime gate (requires sim)
feedback_loops.verify_phase_2: bash exts/aic-dt/scripts/verify_phase_2.sh — Phase 2 runtime gate (requires sim)
feedback_loops.engine_e2e: bash exts/aic-dt/scripts/run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh <trial_id> (requires sim + engine container + zenoh fix)

skills_required_per_iteration: isaac-sim-extension-dev (mandatory before any code touching Isaac Sim 5.0 / OpenUSD / OmniGraph / articulation / sensors / MCP socket / aic-dt internals)
nvidia-suite-docs (mandatory for the same surface — fetches live docs; cached LLM knowledge of Isaac Sim 4.x APIs is routinely stale)

cross_repo_policy.scope: Edits to files OUTSIDE /home/aaugus11/Documents/isaac-sim-mcp — especially ~/Documents/aic and ~/IsaacSim-ros_workspaces — are PERMITTED in service of correctly setting up the Isaac Sim path. No artificial restrictions on which files can be touched.
prohibited: Edits inside ~/Documents/aic that artificially make trials pass without legitimate Isaac Sim integration. Specifically: disabling assertions in CheatCode.py / aic_controller / aic_engine / aic_adapter; relaxing tolerances inside aic source to mask Isaac Sim deltas; mocking out aic_engine checks; bypassing scoring logic. The trials must pass because the Isaac Sim digital twin behaves correctly, not because the verifier was weakened.
cross_repo_policy.tracking: Every edit to a file outside isaac-sim-mcp MUST be appended as one entry to plans/cross_repo_changes.json. Schema per entry: { ts (ISO 8601), task_id, repo_path (absolute), file_path (relative to repo_path), reason (one sentence), reversibility ('reversible' | 'irreversible'), commit_sha (string if committed in that repo, null if uncommitted/dirty), notes (optional) }. The file is created on first cross-repo edit if missing; never overwritten — append-only.

scene_authoring_source_of_truth.principle: AIC's Gazebo configs in ~/Documents/aic/ are the canonical source-of-truth for every scene spawn / launch shortcut we build in Isaac Sim. When authoring a new scene atom, robot init, sensor wiring, or spawn parameter, FIRST consult the AIC Gazebo equivalent and mirror its config — don't invent new defaults.
primary_sources: ~/Documents/aic/aic_description/urdf/ur_gz.urdf.xacro — robot definition, joint drives, sensor frames (FT sensor, wrist cameras), gripper config (Robotiq Hand-E)
~/Documents/aic/aic_bringup/launch/spawn_task_board.launch.py — task-board spawn parameter surface (mount rails, port occupancy, per-entity translation/RPY)
~/Documents/aic/aic_bringup/launch/*.launch.py — all launch entry points (robot, board, cable, scoring) with their canonical defaults
~/Documents/aic/aic_engine/config/sample_config.yaml — per-trial scene state (board pose, rail occupancy, port poses, cable pose, attach state, gripper offset)

Live aic_eval Docker container topic surface — corroborates URDF + launch file config; captured via existing scripts under exts/aic-dt/scripts/snapshot_aic_eval_*.sh and run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh
scene_authoring_source_of_truth.rule: Any time an aic-dt MCP atom or scene-authoring step has a default value (joint stiffness, camera intrinsic, drive damping, prim hierarchy, frame_id, topic name, RPY orientation, mass, density, etc.), the canonical default is whatever Gazebo uses — not Isaac Sim's idiomatic value. If divergence is intentional (e.g., Isaac Sim physics stability requires different damping), document the rationale inline at the code site AND in the commit message citing the live Gazebo source-of-truth value being diverged from.

task_selection_principle: All tasks below are independently grabbable. There are no inter-task gates in this JSON. Pick freely from any task with passes:false based on (1) task priority, (2) your assessment of which task you can complete cleanly in this iteration. Some tasks' descriptions reference other tasks informally (e.g., 'uses the wrapper extended in wrapper-json-emit'); use that as a HINT — if a referenced prerequisite hasn't been done, prefer a task whose path to success is clear. If you pick a task and verify_command fails because of a missing prerequisite, that's a fail-forward signal: do NOT commit, append the diagnostic note to progress.txt, exit; next iteration will pick something else.

tasks.title: Capture all 3 Gazebo trial baselines via AIC pipeline
tasks.description: Operator confirms aic_engine + CheatCode HAS been run against Gazebo on this machine via AIC's pipeline. Investigate how AIC's pipeline launches Gazebo + runs trials (read ~/Documents/aic/aic_bringup/launch/*.launch.py, ~/Documents/aic/aic_engine entrypoints, any docs/scripts/Makefile/pixi tasks under ~/Documents/aic/). Reproduce the bringup; run aic_engine + CheatCode for trial_1, trial_2, trial_3; capture each outcome to exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_<id>__gazebo.json with shape: {trial_id, sim:'gazebo', insertion_event_fired (bool), offlimit_contact_count (int), completed_steps (int), ts (ISO 8601)}. Document the capture mechanism in exts/aic-dt/docs/gazebo-baseline-capture.md so it's reproducible. Two implementation paths: (a) write run_aic_engine_against_gazebo.sh sibling to the Isaac Sim wrapper, sharing the same --output-json shape; (b) discover that AIC's pipeline already produces structured outcomes and adapt them. Pick whichever is cheaper after reading AIC source.
acceptance: exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_1__gazebo.json + trial_2__gazebo.json + trial_3__gazebo.json all exist with the standard JSON shape
Each has insertion_event_fired=true (Gazebo trial baseline under CheatCode is expected to pass — these are the M1 reference outcomes)
exts/aic-dt/docs/gazebo-baseline-capture.md describes the bringup steps + capture command line, enough that a reader could regenerate the baselines
Capture is reproducible: re-running the documented steps produces the same insertion_event/offlimit verdicts (run twice if uncertain)
tasks.verify_command: test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_1__gazebo.json && test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_2__gazebo.json && test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_3__gazebo.json && python3 -c "import json; [json.load(open(f'exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/trial_{i}__gazebo.json')) for i in (1,2,3)]" && test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/gazebo-baseline-capture.md
tasks.notes: Independent — picks up early. Cross-repo touches into ~/Documents/aic likely (read-only or invocation; track any actual edits in plans/cross_repo_changes.json). Hardware reality: running Gazebo concurrently with Isaac Sim may stress the machine — operator may want to kill Isaac Sim before this task runs and relaunch after.

tasks.title: Research spike: AIC Isaac Lab env leverage assessment
tasks.description: AIC ships an Isaac Lab environment somewhere under ~/Documents/aic/. The existing GSD work built on Isaac Sim 5.0 extension-based path (aic-dt) without leveraging AIC's Isaac Lab env at any point. Investigate whether this is a missed leverage point. Steps: (1) locate and read AIC's Isaac Lab env source (find / locate any aic_isaaclab / *_isaaclab / Isaac Lab task definitions under ~/Documents/aic/); (2) catalog reusable assets — USD paths, robot init logic, controller wiring patterns, scoring/contact pipelines, environment registration; (3) compare against what aic-dt currently re-implements; (4) verdict: leverage now (M1) / leverage in M2 / not-applicable (Isaac Lab env serves a different purpose). This is INFORMATIONAL — does not gate any other task; if the verdict is 'leverage now' and re-pointing aic-dt at AIC's Isaac Lab env saves significant work, raise as a blocker note in progress.txt and we re-scope.
acceptance: exts/aic-dt/docs/aic-isaaclab-leverage.md committed with sections: 'Located at' (path or 'not found'), 'What it contains' (catalog), 'Overlap with aic-dt' (matrix of features), 'Verdict' (leverage now / leverage M2 / N/A), 'Rationale'
If verdict is 'leverage now', a follow-up note appended to plans/progress.txt suggesting PRD restructure
tasks.verify_command: test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/aic-isaaclab-leverage.md && grep -q 'Verdict' exts/aic-dt/docs/aic-isaaclab-leverage.md && grep -q 'Located at' exts/aic-dt/docs/aic-isaaclab-leverage.md
tasks.notes: Independent of all sim work. Cheap (30-60min). Picked up early reduces risk of duplicating work AIC already shipped.

tasks.title: Reconcile arm joint drives to URDF defaults
files_likely: exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py (load_robot — set drive params after articulation init)
exts/aic-dt/scripts/probe_joint_drives.py (NEW; reads live drive params from articulation)
tasks.description: Live audit found arm joint drives are inherited from on-disk USD's OEM defaults (stiffness 95→1322 across joints, damping 0.038→0.529, maxForce 28 or 150) which differ from AIC URDF spec (stiffness=2000, damping=100, maxForce=87 per ~/Documents/aic/aic_description/urdf/ur_gz.urdf.xacro). load_robot doesn't set drive params after articulation init. Two steps: (1) cross-check the URDF source-of-truth values (read ur_gz.urdf.xacro joint definitions); (2) call set_gains / drive API on each arm joint after articulation init in load_robot to match URDF. If URDF + live values are intentionally divergent (e.g., OEM defaults give better Isaac Sim physics behavior), document the rationale instead.
acceptance: After quick_start, all 6 arm joint drives match URDF spec values (stiffness=2000 ±5%, damping=100 ±5%, maxForce=87 ±5%) — verified by probe_joint_drives.py
Probe captures pre/post values; commit message includes the diff

If divergence is intentional, exts/aic-dt/docs/joint-drives-rationale.md documents WHY (e.g., simulation stability) with citations to live behavior — and this task closes with passes:true on grounds of intentional divergence (not ignored)
tasks.notes: Audit finding #4. Could affect trial outcomes if PD response on the arm is off — hence runtime-gate category.

tasks.title: Diagnose & fix PARITY-09 motion deficit (≥95% commanded amplitude)
tasks.description: Post-fix b682de1 (joint_indices=[[0..5]] on 4 call sites), shoulder_lift_joint round-trip on +0.10 rad command produces Δ≈0.016 rad — only 16% commanded amplitude reaches /joint_states. Writes reach DOFs but 84% dissipates. Hunt the root cause through this hypothesis ladder: (1) OGN articulation_controller PD residual still pulling toward stale /joint_states target — verify via og.Controller.attribute('inputs:enabled', node).get(); (2) Phase 3 cable activation expanded articulation 6→46 DOFs; cable mass-coupling on arm joints — temporarily SetActive(False) on /World/UR5e/cable, re-run, compare; (3) set_joint_positions vs apply_action(PD target) interaction — log _articulation._physics_view.get_dof_position_targets() post-write to compare commanded vs actual. Apply the fix from the winning hypothesis. If multiple contribute, fix all.
acceptance: Round-trip on +0.10 rad shoulder_lift_joint command produces Δ ≥ 0.095 rad in /joint_states within 2 seconds
Root cause documented in commit message and progress.txt (which hypothesis won, why)
Isaac Sim 5.0 Articulation / OmniGraph behavior cited from live docs (nvidia-suite-docs skill output) in commit message
tasks.verify_recipe: If probe_motion_roundtrip.py doesn't exist yet, write it: subscribe /joint_states; record initial joint position; publish JointMotionUpdate to /aic_controller/joint_commands with shoulder_lift_joint=initial+target; sleep window-seconds; read /joint_states; assert |final - (initial+target)| <= tolerance.
tasks.notes: Reference .planning/phases/04-trial-loader/04-3.5-PLAN.md Task 1 for the full hypothesis ladder.

If those aren't all in place, this task's verify will fail; pick a different task instead.

All 3 trials show insertion_event_fired=true on Isaac Sim AND on Gazebo
All 3 trials show offlimit_contact_count=0 on Isaac Sim AND on Gazebo
parity_report.py exits 0 (all trials match across sims)

tasks.notes: If outcomes diverge, this task FAILS — agent does not flip passes:true. Iteration must investigate which trial diverged + why.

tasks.title: Implement parity_report.py (regenerable side-by-side trial outcome table)
tasks.description: Independent infra task. Build parity_report.py that compares per-trial outcomes between Isaac Sim and Gazebo and produces docs/parity-report.md. Inputs: exts/aic-dt/docs/trial_outcomes/*.json (one per trial-sim combination). Output: markdown table with columns | Trial | Gazebo outcome | Isaac Sim outcome | Match? | Insertion event | Off_limit contacts | Notes |. Each trial_outcomes/<trial_id>__<sim>.json file has shape: {trial_id, sim, insertion_event_fired: bool, offlimit_contact_count: int, completed_steps: int, ts}. Script should: (1) read all .json files in trial_outcomes/, (2) group by trial_id, (3) compare across sims, (4) write markdown table, (5) print summary line + exit code 0 if all trials match across sims, 1 otherwise.
acceptance: exts/aic-dt/scripts/parity_report.py exists and is invokable
Running it with empty trial_outcomes/ produces a graceful warning + exit 1 (no data)
Running it with one mock trial_outcomes/trial_1__gazebo.json + one trial_1__isaacsim.json produces a 1-row markdown table
docs/parity-report.md is regenerable deterministically — running twice with same inputs produces byte-identical output
Script has --help that explains the input file shape
tasks.verify_recipe: Agent writes test_parity_report.sh: creates 2 mock trial_outcomes JSON files, runs parity_report.py, asserts the markdown table contains both, asserts deterministic output (run twice, diff = 0), cleans up mocks.
tasks.notes: Independent of all sim work. Ideal early iteration target.

tasks.title: Verify PARITY-10 pose-cmd round-trip post-fix 922a7d5
files_likely: exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/controller_loop.py (verify only; fix may already be in place)
tasks.description: Pose-cmd round-trip is 100% broken pre-fix; fix 922a7d5 added joint_indices on 5th call site (Lula IK write) but TF tree desynced after hot-reload prevented mid-session re-test. Validate it now. Procedure: clean-restart Isaac Sim with venv activate + ROS_DOMAIN_ID=7; quick_start; subscribe TF base_link→tool0 to capture x0/y0/z0/q0; publish MotionUpdate with pose.position.z = z0 - 0.05, header.frame_id=base_link, mode=POSITION, stiffness=200, damping=20; hold publish at 30Hz for 3s; re-read TF; compute Δ. Informal hint: if motion-deficit-hunt's fix isn't in place, this task may close simultaneously with it — same surface (controller_loop.py).
acceptance: |Δz + 0.05| ≤ 0.005m (≥90% commanded amplitude — Cartesian uses Lula IK, looser tolerance than joint cmd)
Round-trip captured deterministically by probe_pose_roundtrip.py — must be re-runnable without manual steps
tasks.notes: If motion-deficit-hunt closes the OGN articulation_controller fight, this task likely closes simultaneously.

tasks.title: Reconcile 4 task-board prim authoring (RigidBodyAPI + mass=0 + density=0)
files_likely: exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/extension.py OR exts/aic-dt/aic_dt/scoring_publishers.py (depending on where authoring happens)
tasks.description: 4 task-board prims (/World/TaskBoard/base_visual, /World/TaskBoard/SCPort_0/sc_port_visual, /World/TaskBoard/SCPort_1/sc_port_visual, /World/TaskBoard/NICCard/nic_card_visual) have RigidBodyAPI applied AND mass=0 AND density=0. PhysX silently treats them as kinematic. Functional outcome matches Gazebo's bolted-board state, BUT the contact pipeline depends on these being correctly authored as rigid bodies for PhysxContactReportAPI to fire /scoring/insertion_event. Plan 04-03's recursive contact-tag walk fix specifically had to descend into SCPort_0/sc_port_visual because root-only tagging missed rigid-body descendants — so the authoring matters for scoring correctness. Three options: (a) remove RigidBodyAPI entirely (truly static) — simplest, but PhysX may then skip these from contact reports altogether; (b) set kinematicEnabled=true on RigidBodyAPI explicitly — preserves contact reporting, makes intent explicit; (c) give them real mass + density — risks unintended dynamics if anchoring isn't already in place. Use isaac-sim-extension-dev + nvidia-suite-docs skills to check live PhysX 5.0 behavior for kinematic+RigidBodyAPI vs static-no-API contact-report semantics; pick the option that preserves contact firing reliably.
acceptance: All 4 prims authored consistently (one of the 3 options above), with rationale in commit message citing live PhysX 5.0 contact-report behavior from nvidia-suite-docs
After quick_start, /scoring/insertion_event fires when a plug contacts its target port (verified by ros2 topic echo /scoring/insertion_event during a manual plug-port collision via MCP)
Probe script captures the 4 prims' final API state (RigidBodyAPI applied? mass? density? kinematicEnabled?) and logs it; this becomes the authoring contract
tasks.notes: Audit finding #6. Connection to M1: scoring topic fires depend on this. Trial fires need this in place.

If those aren't in place, this task's verify will fail; pick a different task instead.

tasks.title: Extend run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh to emit per-trial outcome JSON
tasks.description: Current wrapper exists from Plan 04-03 but doesn't emit a structured per-trial outcome JSON. Extend it to: (1) accept a --output-json <path> flag; (2) during the engine run, subscribe to /scoring/insertion_event AND /aic/gazebo/contacts/off_limit AND track engine-reported step count; (3) at engine container exit, emit a JSON file matching the parity-report-tool input shape: {trial_id, sim:'isaacsim', insertion_event_fired (bool — true if /scoring/insertion_event ever received during the run), offlimit_contact_count (int — total non-empty Contacts messages on /aic/gazebo/contacts/off_limit), completed_steps (int — final step count from engine stdout or a default sentinel like -1 if not parseable), ts (ISO 8601 of run start)}. Without this, trial-N-fire tasks each have to invent capture logic — bad DRY. Informal prerequisite: zenoh-path-implementation should be done first so the wrapper is exercising a real engine path; otherwise verify will fail because engine can't talk to model.
acceptance: Running `bash exts/aic-dt/scripts/run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh trial_1 --output-json /tmp/test.json` produces /tmp/test.json with all 5 keys present
JSON shape matches parity-report-tool's expected input exactly
Exit code 0 if engine ran successfully; nonzero if engine container errored (capture stays valid even on engine failure — write JSON with whatever was observed before failure)
Backwards-compatible: invoking the wrapper without --output-json behaves exactly as before (emits no JSON, otherwise unchanged)
tasks.verify_recipe: Agent writes test_wrapper_json_emit.sh: invokes wrapper with --output-json on trial_1 (or a stub trial), asserts JSON file exists with required keys, asserts JSON is parseable, asserts the run without --output-json doesn't write the JSON. Cleans up test artifacts.
tasks.notes: Sister script for Gazebo path may be needed in gazebo-baselines-capture — let that task decide whether to extend the same wrapper for --gazebo mode or create a sibling wrapper based on how AIC's pipeline already runs Gazebo trials.

tasks.title: Diagnose & fix PARITY-05 wrench garbage zeros (Articulation lifecycle)
tasks.description: /fts_broadcaster/wrench publishes 13.6Hz with all-zero payload. '[Force callback] Articulation object has no attribute _physics_view' fires every tick despite path-correction commit 6fc6d26 + hasattr guard. Investigation: (1) use nvidia-suite-docs skill to fetch Isaac Sim 5.0 Articulation lifecycle — when does _physics_view populate post-initialize()? Does Articulation (multi-prim) differ from SingleArticulation for prim_paths_expr=root_joint? (2) Compare _lazy_init_articulation in extension.py vs parity_publishers.py:_on_physics_step lazy init — parity_publishers WORKS for /joint_states (positions read fine), find the divergence in init order or method-access pattern. (3) If a fundamental gap exists requiring a workaround pattern (manual physics-view reconstruction, alternate Articulation subclass), document the workaround clearly — don't claim full closure if fragile.
acceptance: /fts_broadcaster/wrench publishes nonzero force/torque on idle gravity-loaded robot (idle weight should produce nonzero z-axis)
Zero `[Force callback] AttributeError` lines in ~/.nvidia-omniverse/logs/Kit/'Isaac-Sim Full'/5.0/kit_*.log over a 30-second window after quick_start
Diagnostic trail captured in commit message + progress.txt for upstream nvidia-suite-docs contribution (Articulation lifecycle pitfalls)
If solution is a workaround (not root-cause fix), label it as such honestly; verdict tier = root-caused / workaround / diagnosed-but-unsolvable
tasks.notes: aic_adapter consumes via Observation.wrist_wrench → reaches model. Without this, every CheatCode trial gets garbage wrench and may behave unpredictably. Parity_publishers gives a known-good lazy-init reference.

tasks.title: Author 3 wrist Camera prims + wire setup_wrist_cameras + enable_cameras kwarg (default False during ship-gate)
files_likely: exts/aic-dt/assets/usds/aic_unified_robot.usda (or wherever the unified robot USD lives)
exts/aic-dt/docs/wrist-cameras.md (NEW; documents the 3 frame_ids + topics + ROS-frame quaternion convention)
tasks.description: Currently zero camera topics publish. setup_wrist_cameras logs 'Setup 0/3' every quick_start because the WRIST_CAMERAS prim_suffix expects center_camera_optical/center_camera (Camera child of Xform) but the live unified-robot USD has center_camera_optical as Xform with only visuals/collisions children — NO Camera prims authored anywhere. Three steps: (1) author 3 Camera prims under existing wrist Xform parents (left_camera_optical/left_camera, center_camera_optical/center_camera, right_camera_optical/right_camera) with ROS-frame -Z-forward → +X-forward orientation correction (per nvidia-suite-docs Isaac Sim 5.0 Camera prim authoring + isaac-sim-extension-dev wrist-camera attachment pattern); (2) verify setup_wrist_cameras now logs 'Setup 3/3' and publishes /wrist_camera_left/image_raw, /wrist_camera_center/image_raw, /wrist_camera_right/image_raw (or whatever the Gazebo-faithful topic names are — capture from aic_eval Docker before authoring); (3) add enable_cameras: bool=False kwarg on quick_start, gate setup_wrist_cameras call on it, default False so parity-report-run ship-gate runs without camera bandwidth (Docker engine consumes ground_truth, not vision). Operator can flip to True for vision testing.
acceptance: Calling quick_start(enable_cameras=True) results in 3 camera topics publishing (verified by ros2 topic list | grep -c camera = 3)
All 3 cameras stream at >5Hz with valid sensor_msgs/Image payloads (non-empty data field; width/height nonzero)
Calling quick_start(enable_cameras=False) — the default — results in zero camera topics publishing (verified by ros2 topic list | grep camera = empty)
setup_wrist_cameras atom 4-surface contract preserved (registry + handler + _cmd + UI button); audit_dx02.py exits 0
exts/aic-dt/docs/wrist-cameras.md documents the 3 frame_ids, topic names, ROS-frame quaternion convention, and the rationale for default-off
Topic names match Gazebo's wrist-camera topic surface (capture from aic_eval container before authoring; document in wrist-cameras.md as Gazebo-faithful evidence)
tasks.verify_recipe: Script: (1) call quick_start(enable_cameras=False) via MCP; assert ros2 topic list shows 0 wrist-camera topics; (2) call quick_start(enable_cameras=True); assert 3 camera topics with rate >5Hz; (3) ros2 topic echo --once on each, assert non-empty data + nonzero width/height; (4) exit 0 only if both branches pass.
tasks.notes: Absorbs Phase 1 Plan 01-G05's setup_wrist_cameras prim path correction. Cross-repo touch likely: capturing Gazebo topic names requires aic_eval container, which is read-only for this purpose.

tasks.title: Research & decide model-zenoh transport path
tasks.description: my-solution:v1 (CheatCode policy container) requires zenoh transport (AIC_ROUTER_ADDR=localhost:7447 + RMW_IMPLEMENTATION=rmw_zenoh_cpp hard-required). Host humble fastrtps engine cannot discover /aic_model/get_state across this transport boundary. Three paths exist (a/b/c). BEFORE picking, INVESTIGATE WHY AIC's pixi setup chose zenoh in the first place: read ~/Documents/aic/aic_eval Dockerfile + entrypoint, ~/Documents/aic README/docs, any DDS/zenoh comments in pyproject.toml or pixi.toml. The team had a reason — probably (1) discovery scaling (DDS multicast doesn't play in cloud/k8s), (2) named-resource service matching, or (3) interop with kilted-only packages. Whichever it is matters for path-correctness: if zenoh was chosen for X reason, a fastdds bridge that handles X loses architectural fidelity. Output a markdown verdict with: (1) WHY zenoh was chosen (citations), (2) which of the 3 paths preserves that reason, (3) impl steps for the chosen path. Path candidates: (a) host zenohd + fastdds↔zenoh bridge ~30min; (b) host-build aic_model humble-native ~60-90min; (c) stub /aic_model/get_state lifecycle node ~20min — discriminator only.
acceptance: exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-decision.md committed with the 3 sections: 'Why zenoh' (with citations to AIC source/docs), 'Path verdict' (a/b/c with rationale), 'Implementation plan' (concrete steps for the next task zenoh-path-implementation)
Verdict is operator-reviewable — at least one paragraph of architectural reasoning, not just a one-line answer
If neither (a) nor (b) is clearly cheaper, the doc says so and recommends (c) as a discriminator-only smoke test before committing to (b)
tasks.verify_command: test -f exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-decision.md && grep -q 'Why zenoh' exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-decision.md && grep -q 'Path verdict' exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-decision.md && grep -q 'Implementation plan' exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-decision.md
tasks.notes: Operator preference: rebuild humble-native is fine if not a multi-file rewrite; bridge is fine if it preserves architectural fidelity. Decision belongs to research, not preference.

tasks.title: Resolve service-RPC stall through zenoh router (humble engine ↔ kilted model)
files_likely: Possibly aic_eval Docker image env (AIC_ROUTER_ADDR, ROS_DOMAIN_ID, RMW config)
tasks.description: zenoh-path-implementation closed on transport (router lifecycle + topic discovery + service-name discovery all green) but service-RPC PAYLOADS stall through zenoh between humble engine and kilted model. Symptom (3 trial-fire progress.txt entries confirm identical signature): engine logs `Service '/aic_model/get_state' is available. Participant model discovered.` then GetState→ChangeState→SwitchController RPC timeouts → trial never executed → insertion_event_fired stays false. THIS IS THE M1 SHIP BLOCKER — parity-report-run fails by design until this resolves. Hypothesis ladder (investigate via nvidia-suite-docs Isaac ROS sub-skill + ROS Discourse + zenoh community for kilted RMW quirks): (1) DDS↔zenoh service-payload bridge gap — zenoh router may not relay service req/resp on default config; check zenoh service-discovery flags + REST hooks. (2) kilted-rmw-zenoh ↔ humble-rmw-zenoh ABI mismatch on rcl_action / rcl_lifecycle service descriptors — check humble_ws install for msg version drift. (3) ROS_DOMAIN_ID gating within zenoh networks (zenoh shouldn't care but kilted's rmw_zenoh impl might still gate). (4) AIC_ROUTER_ADDR pointing wrong — engine container connects to host:7447 but model container expects internal:7447. Investigation is iterative — document findings as they emerge in zenoh-rpc-stall-fix.md, apply the fix once root-caused. If root cause is config-only, fix lives in run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh / sim launcher. If it requires aic_model rebuild or aic source patching, log to plans/cross_repo_changes.json per cross-repo policy. After this lands: trial-1/2/3-fire need to be re-fired to capture corrected outcomes; parity-report-run flips green.
acceptance: Running run_aic_engine_against_isaac_sim.sh trial_1 --output-json /tmp/test.json results in trial_1.json with insertion_event_fired=true OR clean engine-side completion (no GetState/ChangeState RPC timeouts in engine stdout)

exts/aic-dt/docs/zenoh-rpc-stall-fix.md committed with sections: 'Symptom' (verbatim engine log excerpt), 'Root cause' (citation to nvidia-suite-docs / Isaac ROS / zenoh source), 'Fix applied' (specific config/code change), 'Verification' (reproducible command)
Any system config / cross-repo edits logged in plans/cross_repo_changes.json with reversibility flag
test_zenoh_rpc_stall_fix.sh exists and exits 0 — re-runs trial_1 fire end-to-end, asserts no RPC timeout
tasks.verify_recipe: Agent writes test_zenoh_rpc_stall_fix.sh: re-runs trial_1 fire end-to-end via wrapper, asserts engine completed without RPC timeout (grep engine stdout for 'GetState timeout' / 'ChangeState timeout'), asserts insertion_event_fired captured real outcome (not stall artifact). Cleans up test JSON.
tasks.notes: M1 ship critical path. Once this passes: agent flips trial-1/2/3-fire back to passes:false on next iter (or operator does manually) → trials re-fire with working transport → parity-report-run closes → ship-paperwork.

tasks.title: Fix engine StopRecording WaitForTfs timeout at trial-end
tasks.description:
Promoted from progress.txt DEFERRED-5 (surfaced post-DEFERRED-4). After CheatCode runs the trial for its full 180s task time_limit, the engine calls scoring_tier2_->StopRecording() which itself calls WaitForTfs() (aic_scoring/src/ScoringTier2.cc ~line 153). This second WaitForTfs times out, producing:
  [ERROR] Timeout while waiting for transforms for scoring.
  [ERROR] Failed to stop recording.
Which blocks tier_2/tier_3 scoring from finalizing (the bag isn't cleanly closed → scoring playback gets incomplete data → trial marked 'failed or was not completed' even when CheatCode ran for the full time_limit).

Hypothesis ladder:
  H1) Clock-source mismatch — engine runs with use_sim_time=true; Isaac Sim's rclpy publishers (parity_publishers, scoring_publishers, controller_loop) use default ROS_TIME. WaitForTfs's spinlock uses node->get_clock()->now() which respects use_sim_time. If sim time pauses (e.g. trial-end timeline state), engine clock stalls indefinitely while rclpy publishers keep emitting messages — but the engine subscriber might be doing message-timestamp filtering that drops them. Fix: set use_sim_time=true on the Isaac Sim rclpy nodes so they publish with sim-clock timestamps matching engine expectation.
  H2) /scoring/tf went silent during the trial (transient zenoh queryable drop or scoring publisher node death). Probe post-trial showed it publishing at 79Hz, but it could have stalled mid-trial. Fix: add a watchdog log line in _publish_scoring_tf to surface any tick-skip.
  H3) Engine's WaitForTfs subscribes to /tf + /scoring/tf at StartRecording-time then resets flags and waits at StopRecording-time — but the subscriber QoS or RMW state may have decayed across the trial duration. Fix: investigate engine subscriber lifecycle.

Fix-applied verification (acceptance bullets below) is the gate: trial_1 must complete with engine logging 'Engine Stopped' without 'Timeout while waiting for transforms for scoring'. tier_2/tier_3 SCORE values may still be 0 (DEFERRED-6 physics) but the failure mode must move from 'infrastructure' to 'task execution failed' (legitimate scoring assessment of the policy's motion).
acceptance: Engine log shows 'Successfully stopped recording' OR equivalent — no 'Failed to stop recording.' line at trial_1 end
Zero 'Timeout while waiting for transforms for scoring' lines in engine stdout
Trial outcome JSON captured by wrapper reflects actual task execution (insertion_event_fired may still be false due to DEFERRED-6, but completed_steps reflects real motion ticks)
Test script test_scoring_stoprecording_fix.sh exists and exits 0
tasks.verify_recipe: test_scoring_stoprecording_fix.sh: re-run trial_1 via wrapper; assert zero 'Timeout while waiting for transforms for scoring' lines in /tmp/aic_engine_isaac.log; assert 'Failed to stop recording' is absent; assert outcome JSON written + parseable. Cleans up test artifacts.
tasks.notes: After this closes: parity_report.py shows real Isaac-vs-Gazebo scoring divergence (not infrastructure-stalled artifacts). Then DEFERRED-6 (cable physics + controller motion fidelity, Phase 3 SCENE-05/PARITY-09 territory) is the last layer before tier_2/tier_3 actually pass.
